lesson.menu_title: Building Trust -- Networking and Professional Relationships
lesson.subtitle: Aula 11 -- Building Trust -- Networking
lesson.title_tag: Professor View -- Natalie Viegas | Aula 11 -- Building Trust

listenings.text: Hi Natalie, this is Tom Bradley. It was great to meet you at the tech conference yesterday. We had a really good rapport, didn't we? I think we have a mutual interest in cloud solutions. I wanted to reach out because a colleague of mine is looking for a reliable partner, and I would like to give you a referral. You made a genuine impression, and your strong results give you real credibility. Let us nurture this connection over time. You are very approachable, so I feel comfortable asking: could we have a short call next week? You are free on Thursday, aren't you? Please let me know. Thanks, and talk soon.

Hi, this is Natalie, recording a quick reflection after the tech conference. At first, networking felt difficult, but it went well. I met Tom, a new acquaintance, and we built a good rapport very quickly. We talked about a mutual interest in cloud solutions. He said that he would send me a referral, which gives me more credibility in a new market. I tried to be genuine and approachable, not just to collect business cards. The real work starts now. I have to reach out, follow up, and nurture these relationships over time. A strong network is built on trust, and trust takes time. I think I made a reliable first impression, and I feel more confident about networking now.

extra_audio.text: First, you start a genuine conversation and find a mutual interest. Then you exchange contacts and agree to reach out. After that, you follow up soon, while the connection is still fresh. Next, you offer real value, like a referral or useful information. Finally, you nurture the relationship over time, so it stays reliable.
